etymology.description: Depicts silk rope for binding objects; original form of 紀 (紀) (jì, "record")
etymology.interpretation: 己 (jǐ, "self") depicts a silk rope for binding, symbolizing the concept of self-containment or self-restriction, leading to the abstract sense of 'self'.

examples.chinese: 知己知彼(知己知彼)，百战不殆(百戰不殆)。
examples.pinyin: Zhī jǐ zhī bǐ, bǎi zhàn bù dài.
examples.english: Know yourself and know your enemy, and you will never be defeated.

examples.chinese: 每个人都应该反省自己(每個人都應該反省自己)。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè rén dōu yīng gāi fǎn xǐng zì jǐ.

examples.chinese: 你要为自己负责(你要為自己負責)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ yào wèi zì jǐ fù zé.
examples.english: You need to be responsible for yourself.
